steps.title: Use the telescope facts
steps.content: The passage says HST has already produced important visible and ultraviolet images of solar-system bodies, but only about 6% of the bodies it has imaged are inside the solar system. Kim and colleagues recommend a new telescope dedicated exclusively to solar-system observations, which means they want a different instrument to take on that specialized work.

steps.title: Reject unsupported claims
steps.content: Choice B overstates the result by saying HST will no longer observe solar-system objects. Choice C invents a modification to HST, which the passage never mentions. Choice D claims HST lacks needed sensors, but the passage says HST already works in visible and UV wavelengths.

steps.title: Choose the implied role
steps.content: Since the new telescope would specialize in solar-system surveys and long-term UV observations, HST would likely continue in its current broader role, primarily observing objects outside the solar system. The answer is A.
